evals.name: hook-lint-on-edit
evals.prompt: Add a hook to my Claude Code settings so that whenever Claude edits or writes any file, it automatically runs `npm run lint --fix 2>&1` in the project root. Show me the exact JSON to add to my ~/.claude/settings.json.
evals.expected_output: A complete, correct hooks configuration showing PostToolUse event with Edit|Write matcher and a command handler running npm run lint --fix

assertions.description: Response uses the PostToolUse hook event (not PreToolUse)
Matcher covers both Edit and Write tools
Uses $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR or equivalent to run in project root
The JSON snippet is syntactically valid and uses the correct hooks nesting structure
Does not invent non-existent hook fields or events

evals.name: permissions-git-allow-deny
evals.prompt: Configure my Claude Code settings to allow git read commands (status, log, diff, show) but block destructive git operations like push, reset --hard, and force-push. Show me the exact permissions config.
evals.expected_output: Correct allow rules for safe git read commands and deny rules for destructive git operations using proper Bash() rule syntax

assertions.description: Includes allow rules covering git status, git log, git diff, and/or git show
Includes a deny rule that blocks git push
Includes a deny rule that blocks git reset --hard or git reset
Uses Bash(...) rule syntax correctly (not just strings like 'git push')
Response shows awareness that deny takes precedence over allow (evaluation order)

evals.name: voice-and-model-config
evals.prompt: I want to enable voice dictation in tap mode with auto-submit turned on, and set my default model to claude-sonnet-4-6. Show me the complete config snippet for my settings.json.
evals.expected_output: Correct voice object (not deprecated voiceEnabled) plus model setting in valid JSON

assertions.description: Uses the voice object {enabled, mode, autoSubmit} rather than the deprecated voiceEnabled boolean
Uses the model key (not modelId or defaultModel) set to claude-sonnet-4-6
The snippet is valid JSON that could be merged into settings.json
